name: loom
description: Persistent symbol index for AI coding agents. Tree-sitter indexes your codebase into SQLite. Agents search by name/keyword and get summaries + signatures instantly — 51–1507× fewer tokens than reading files. Agent-written summaries persist across sessions: every run makes Loom smarter. Zero LLM cost. No Docker.

LOOM_DB_PATH.description: Override the SQLite database path. Optional — Loom auto-detects per-project DB from git root.

tools.name: search_code
tools.description: FTS5 full-text search across all indexed symbols. Returns summary + signature if cached — agent may skip file read entirely.

tools.name: get_context
tools.description: Full context packet for one function: summary, signature, callers (top 10), callees (top 10), staleness flag.

tools.name: store_understanding
tools.description: Write agent-generated summary back to the index permanently. Skips write if summary already fresh.

tools.name: get_blast_radius
tools.description: Transitive callers of a function — what breaks if this changes.

tools.name: get_delta
tools.description: What changed since last session. Only read changed functions, skip the rest.

tools.name: start_session
tools.description: Register session start, returns session_id for use with get_delta.

tools.name: god_nodes
tools.description: Most-called functions in the repo — good entry points for exploration.

tools.name: graph_stats
tools.description: Node and edge counts by kind — fast repo overview.

resources.description: ~200-token codebase overview: modules, hot functions, summary coverage, last analyzed. Load at session start.

prerequisites: Run `loom analyze .` in your repo before connecting
setup_instructions:
1. Install: `pip install loom-tool`
2. Index your repo: `cd loom && loom analyze .`
3. Configure all AI tools: `loom install`
4. Connect this MCP server — Claude Code will load `loom://primer` automatically at session start.
